title: Issue Distribution by Device and Site
dataSources: `index=catalyst sourcetype=cisco:dnac:issue`. from the `issue` modular input in the Cisco Catalyst Add-on (Splunkbase 7538) polling `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/issues`. Default poll interval: 900s (15m). Volume: ~1 event per active issue per poll. RBAC: NETWORK-ADMIN-ROLE. Key fields: `priority` (string), `category` (string), `name` (string), `deviceName` (string), `status` (string), `issueId` (string), `siteId` (string), `lastOccurenceTime` (epoch milliseconds).
spl: index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:issue" | stats count as issue_count dc(name) as unique_issues by deviceId, siteId | sort -issue_count | head 20
description: Maps assurance issues to specific devices and sites to identify problem hotspots in the network infrastructure.
value: Some devices or sites generate disproportionate issue volumes. Identifying these hotspots focuses remediation where it has the most impact.
implementation: Enable the `issue` input. Confirm `siteId` is extracted from Catalyst Center issue payloads. If the field is missing, enrich using a site lookup or device inventory from Catalyst Center inventory feeds in Splunk.
visualization: Table (top 20 by issue_count), treemap or packed bubble by siteId and deviceId.

references.title: Catalyst Center Issues API — Cisco DevNet

knownFalsePositives:
**Single device with a persistent issue inflating that device's issue count.** A device with an unresolved bug or configuration issue will generate an issue event on every poll, making it appear as the top issue generator. Distinguish by checking whether the high `issue_count` for a device corresponds to a single `issueId` that persists across polls. Suppress by using `dc(issueId)` as the metric rather than event count.

**Lab or PoC devices generating issues that are not operationally relevant.** Test equipment may generate issues that inflate the per-device distribution without warranting operational attention. Distinguish by checking whether `deviceId` corresponds to a known lab device. Suppress by filtering lab devices from the distribution using a `catalyst_excluded_devices` lookup.

**Site hierarchy change reassigning devices and issues to different siteId values.** When the site hierarchy is reorganized, issues for the same device may appear under a different `siteId`, distorting the per-site distribution. Distinguish by correlating with `index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:audit:logs"` for site hierarchy changes. Suppress by tracking by `deviceId` (stable) rather than `siteId` (may change).

**Cross-product issues showing the correlating device as the source.** Issues detected through cross-product correlation (e.g., ISE authentication failure affecting a Catalyst Center-managed device) may be attributed to the device rather than the external system. Distinguish by checking the issue `category` — cross-product issues often have specific category names. Do not suppress — the attribution is correct but the root cause may be in the external system.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• **issue** events with **`deviceId`**; **`siteId`** is required for this **split**—if missing, **join** to **`cisco:dnac:device`** or a **CMDB/lookup** keyed by `deviceId` or serial (see other Catalyst UCs in this family).
• Cisco Catalyst Add-on 7538; confirm **field** names on raw JSON (some builds nest **location** under **site**).
• `docs/implementation-guide.md` for **lookup** placement in **`lookups/`** and **transforms**.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• **API:** `GET /dna/intent/api/v1/issues` (same **issue** input as **UC-5.13.21**).
• **Key fields for this view:** `deviceId`, `siteId`, `name` (issue title).
• **Enrichment** when **`siteId` is null:** **`| lookup catal_site_lookup deviceId OUTPUT siteId`** (example) after you build the table from inventory or **device** **health** data.

Step 2 — Create the report
```spl
index=catalyst sourcetype="cisco:dnac:issue" | stats count as issue_count dc(name) as unique_issues by deviceId, siteId | sort -issue_count | head 20
```

Understanding this SPL (breadth vs volume)
• **`dc(name)`** is **how many** **different** **Assurance** titles hit that **device**—**high** **issue_count** with **low** **unique_issues** is **stutter**; **high** on **both** is **broad** failure.
• **`head 20`:** change to **50** for large retailers; for **trellis** by **region**, add **post-process** filters.

**Pipeline walkthrough**
• **Hotspot** table for **war-room** wall: pair with **Catalyst** **Device 360** links using **`deviceId`** in the **drilldown** token.

Step 3 — Validate
• **Cross-check** top **`deviceId`** in **Catalyst** against **this device’s** open issues; **count** mismatch usually means **dedup** policy differs.
• **`| stats dc(siteId)`** to ensure **hierarchy** **coverage**; **all NULL** `siteId` means you must **enrich** before trusting the table.

Step 4 — Operationalize
• **Dashboard:** **table** + optional **treemap** (**site** parent, **device** child) in Dashboard Studio if cardinality allows.
• **Hand-off:** assign **RFO** for **#1** **site** each week in ops review—link **UC-5.13.25** for **chronic** **titles**.

• **Validate:** compare against the Catalyst Center Assurance Issues dashboard — verify P1/P2 counts match for the same time window.
Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Blank `deviceId`:** some **global** or **control-plane** issues are not tied to a **single** **device**—handle with a **'N/A'** **bucket** or **separate** **panel**.
• **Duplicate** **rows** for same **chassis** different **line cards:** normalize to **deviceName** in a v2 if your TA also sends **name** **fields** **per** **member** **switch**.
• **No issue events arriving:** verify the `issue` input is enabled and check `index=_internal` for API error or endpoint timeout messages from the TA.
• **Missing fields or empty values:** some issue fields vary by Catalyst Center version; run `| fieldsummary` to identify NULL or zero values that need `fillnull`.
• **Fewer events than the Catalyst Center UI shows:** the API returns only active issues by default; if credential access is denied, check the service account RBAC role.
grandmaExplanation: We rank the noisiest boxes and the sites they sit in, so you send engineers to the exact trouble spots instead of guessing where to look first.
